# Boses by Temujin Technologies — Full Reference

This file is the long-form companion to /llms.txt. It contains Q&A pairs, methodology notes, and detailed product context for AI-search engines that prefer richer source material.

## About Boses

Boses ("voices" in Filipino) is an AI-powered consumer-research simulation platform built by Temujin Technologies. It is purpose-built for Southeast Asia: every persona is grounded in on-the-ground ethnographic fieldwork from one of six markets — Philippines, Indonesia, Vietnam, Thailand, Malaysia, Singapore — rather than translated from generic Western archetypes.

The platform was founded on a simple observation: market research in Southeast Asia is broken. Traditional focus groups cost ₱500,000+ per study, take 6-12 weeks, and routinely produce findings that don't reflect how real Filipinos, Indonesians, or Vietnamese actually buy. Boses replaces that with AI personas you can run a simulation against in hours.

## Who Boses Is For

- **Product managers** at SEA-first companies validating concepts before build
- **Marketers** stress-testing campaign creative before paid-media spend
- **Strategy teams** sizing market opportunities and quantifying preferences
- **Founders** building case for fundraising with consumer-insight evidence

## How a Boses Simulation Works

1. Upload a product brief, concept, or stimulus (PDF, image, text).
2. Select a persona cohort from one of six SEA markets, or upload your own roster.
3. Pick a research method: Concept Test, IDI (AI-assisted or manual), Survey, Focus Group, or Conjoint.
4. Run the simulation — typically 5-30 minutes wall-clock depending on cohort size.
5. Receive a report with sentiment scores, verbatim quotes, cross-persona analysis, and shareable public links.

## Q&A — Long-form

**Q: What's the actual evidence Boses personas reflect real Southeast Asian consumers?**
A: Boses runs ongoing validation studies comparing AI persona output against real human in-depth interview data. The methodology: take a real-world IDI study, generate matched AI persona responses to the same script, then score theme-overlap. Boses publishes the methodology and re-run instructions inside the platform.

**Q: How is Boses different from existing AI-research tools?**
A: Three differences. (1) Geographic focus — Boses is SEA-only, every persona is grounded in local fieldwork. Generic AI research tools default to Western archetypes that don't reflect halal preferences, family-purchasing dynamics, or specific channel behaviors like LINE-in-Thailand or Gojek-in-Indonesia. (2) Full method coverage — Boses supports all six major qualitative/quantitative research methods on one platform. (3) Shareable outputs — Boses report links work without login, so non-platform stakeholders can view findings directly.

**Q: Can Boses run validated quantitative research?**
A: Yes for descriptive quant (surveys, conjoint, preference distributions). Boses outputs include per-question distributions, segment breakdowns, and verbatim explanations. For inferential statistics with significance testing against real-world population parameters, Boses recommends combining simulation runs with smaller, targeted real-human validation panels.

**Q: What's the data privacy posture?**
A: All persona data is synthetic — no real consumer PII is stored or processed in simulations. Customer-uploaded briefings are encrypted at rest, scoped to the uploading organization, and never used to train upstream language models. Boses uses the OpenAI API as its sole LLM provider.

**Q: How do the free demos work?**
A: Two demos, both at no-account-required: the **persona generator** at https://www.temujintechnologies.com/personas (pick market + describe audience + work email → 3 AI personas in 20-30 seconds), and the **concept test** at https://www.temujintechnologies.com/concept-test (same flow + paste or upload your concept → 3 personas PLUS each one's verbatim reaction PLUS an aggregate analysis with sentiment distribution, top themes, and recommended actions). The concept test supports image and PDF uploads (PNG/JPG/WebP up to 5 MB; PDFs are rendered to first-page PNG) and uses a vision model when an image is attached. Both demos are rate-limited to 3 generations per IP per day, 100 globally per day. Output is permalinked so prospects can share with stakeholders.

**Q: How are the two demos different?**
A: The persona generator answers "what do AI personas for my audience look like?" — it's a quality / realism demo. The concept test goes one step further and answers "do AI simulations actually produce useful insight?" — by running those personas through a real research method (concept test) against the prospect's actual material. The concept test uses the same prompt + methodology as the full Boses platform's Concept Test research method, just scaled to 3 personas instead of a typical 8-12.

**Q: What does pricing look like?**
A: Currently in early access / pre-launch. Pricing tiers depend on simulation volume and seat count and are discussed during the 30-minute walkthrough call. The free demo is and will remain free.

**Q: Can Boses generate personas for markets outside Southeast Asia?**
A: Not currently. Boses' positioning and quality depend on the depth of its SEA-specific fieldwork. Expansion is on the roadmap but gated on completing analogous ethnographic groundwork in each new market.

**Q: How are Boses personas structured?**
A: Each persona has 15 fields: name, age, gender, location, occupation, income level, family situation, personality traits, day-in-life narrative, pain points, aspirational identity, digital behavior (named apps/platforms), buying triggers, values, behavioral insight. All fields are filled with concrete local detail.

## Methodology Notes

- Boses' synthetic-persona generation uses a two-pass LLM pipeline (skeleton + expansion) with reasoning-effort tuning to balance cost and quality.
- Personas are grounded by retrieving matched-archetype fieldwork artifacts from a curated library of SEA ethnographic interviews.
- Simulation outputs are validated against the same fieldwork via a critique pipeline that flags responses straying from market-realistic behavior.
- Methodology re-run is reproducible from inside the platform; the validation suite tracks deltas as the underlying LLMs evolve.
